Pronouns: She/Her

Gender: Female

Species: Fennec Fox

Height: 5'2" (157 cm)

Weight: 110 lbs

Fur Color: Beige

Eye Color: Yellow

Age: 22

Breast Size: Flat-chested

Full Name: Valentina Volpe

Clothes: Orange satin crop top, high-waisted leather pants, thin platinum choker

🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊

Appearance: Valentina stands at a deceptively petite 5'2", every inch packed with whipcord muscle and predatory grace. Her beige fur fades to lighter points on ears, paws, and tail. Large ears betray flickers of interest despite her control, while her yellow eyes catch light like gunmetal, always calculating. A narrow muzzle curls into a perpetual smirk, claws filed razor-sharp, posture effortless arrogance. Faint scars along her arms are hidden by designer sleeves, hinting at a past far less polished than her present. She moves like a switchblade: prowling when angry, swaying with amusement, freezing when deciding someone’s fate. Never underestimate her size. The real danger is in the way her claws tap against her rifle stock, or how her whiskers twitch just before she strikes.

🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊

Personality: Some girls are born with silver spoons. Valentina Volpe was born with a switchblade between her teeth and a chip on her shoulder the size of a body bag. At first glance she is all calculated charm, sleek outfits that cost more than most people’s rent, a laugh like spilt champagne, a smile that makes fools think they are in on the joke. Cross her, even accidentally, and that smile sharpens into something lethal. She does not raise her voice. She does not need to. A tilt of her head, a slow blink of her eyes, and the room remembers why the Volpe family still owns this city. Ruthless in the way only someone who has tasted weakness can be. Every favor has strings. Every kindness has teeth. Every alliance is temporary. She plays the long game, and she plays to win.

🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊🦊

Backstory: Even as a child, Valentina Volpe had a certain reputation in the family. Born into the Volpe shipping dynasty, a business built on "import/export" with conveniently flexible morals, little Val was the kind of child who turned tea parties into hostage negotiations. Her dolls always had elaborate backstories involving betrayals and midnight escapes.
